_metadata.taxonomy_description: Hierarchical taxonomy of inter-individual differences with documented relevance to susceptibility to cyber-manipulation of political opinion. Variables are selected for direct mappability to standard social-science survey instruments (ESS, Eurobarometer, ANES, GSS, British Social Attitudes). The tree mixes (a) continuous trait inventories with facet substructure, (b) absolute numeric scalars, and (c) categorical single-select dimensions. Structure is discovered generically by the pipeline: no inventory names are hardcoded.

inventory_rationales.Big_Five: Canonical personality structure (NEO-PI-R facet resolution). Repeatedly implicated as a moderator of persuasibility and misinformation endorsement.
inventory_rationales.Socioeconomic_Status: Income, employment, and subjective class position. Economic precarity is consistently associated with susceptibility to populist and conspiratorial messaging; directly mappable to ESS/ANES income quintile and employment-type questions.
inventory_rationales.Political_Psychology: Ideological positioning (left-right scale, extremity, partisan strength), institutional trust, and conspiracy belief propensity. These are the primary psychological mediators of susceptibility to politically-targeted disinformation; standard measures in ESS (left-right scale, political trust battery) and Eurobarometer.
inventory_rationales.Social_Context: Online platform behavior, social network characteristics, and interpersonal trust. Platform use and echo-chamber exposure are proximal moderators of information operations; mappable to Pew Research Internet surveys, ESS social trust items, and IAB media-use panels.
